_comment: Loop-progress overlay - written by the port loop, never by hand-editing ledger.json. The verifier records APPLIED here; the applier/verifier record ESCALATED and attempts here. build-ledger.mjs applies it so ledger.json is fully derived (diffs + splits.json + planning/*-decisions.json + progress.json). Each item entry: state (PENDING|APPLIED|ESCALATED - never NO-COUNTERPART, which is planning-frozen), attempts (int), verifierNotes (string[]), escalation (object|null, required when ESCALATED), hunkHash (the item hunkHash when the entry was recorded - if the diff later drifts, the stale entry is ignored).

Note actual port site is resolveSimulationParams, not the planning-hint MNAEngine.init; same function-group semantics (TRANinit copies .tran params + derives CKTmaxStep), structurally faithful.

REOPENED — prior MATCH retracted by user-directed audit against ngspice CAPacLoad as the canonical reference (not inductor.ts). Two structural divergences remain at capacitor.ts:458-482 vs ref/ngspice/src/spicelib/devices/cap/capacld.c:16-40: (1) Function-boundary divergence (Tier-2 bijective): ngspice splits the work between CAPsetup (capsetup.c:114-117 TSTALLOC x4 — allocates the four (posPos, negNeg, posNeg, negPos) matrix cells exactly once) and CAPacLoad (capacld.c:16-40 — zero allocation, pure stamp on the four pre-allocated double* matrix pointers via *(...Ptr+1) ±= m*val). digiTS currently allocates the cells in setup() via allocNortonStamp (returning _handles[0..3]) AND has a 6-line `if (this._hAcPosPos === -1) { ...4x allocElement... }` lazy-alloc block in stampAc (capacitor.ts:469-474) that has no counterpart in CAPacLoad. VERIFICATION.md sec2 Tier-2: a digiTS construct with no v41 counterpart is MISMATCH. (2) Insertion-order divergence (harness instrumentation): sparse-solver.ts:472-474 pushes one _insertionOrder entry per allocElement call regardless of whether the (row,col) already exists; the lazy-alloc block produces 4 extra entries (8 total cap entries vs ngspice's 4), which harness_topology_diff would see as a structural divergence in the per-element-pool order. The matrix shape itself matches ngspice (allocElement on duplicate row,col returns the same handle via the diag fast-path at :451-454 or _spcFindElementInCol find-or-create at :456), so the AC harness paired tests do not fail — but the function-boundary and insertion-order both differ. Required fix: drop the four _hAcPosPos/_hAcNegNeg/_hAcPosNeg/_hAcNegPos fields and the lazy-alloc block; stamp through the existing _handles[0..3] tuple already returned by allocNortonStamp in setup(). This restores ngspice's one-allocation-in-setup, pure-stamp-in-CAPacLoad shape line-for-line. The cap-stampac.md spec needs the parallel update.

BUT TIER 2 FAILS: the functionGroup is jfetacld.c::JFETacLoad (JFET AC small-signal load path), and the whole JFETacLoad function HAS NO digiTS COUNTERPART. NJFETElement implements no stampAc method (search stampAc in njfet.ts = 0 matches), whereas the AC analysis driver calls element.stampAc(solver,omega,loadCtx,rhsRe,rhsIm) per element (ac-analysis.ts:376 runByDeviceFamily('stampAc',...)) and elements without stampAc are SILENTLY SKIPPED (default-loaders.ts:60-61). ngspice's JFET DEVacLoad is JFETacLoad (NOT NULL), so the 'NULL implies skip' equivalence does not apply: a JFET in an AC sweep currently contributes ZERO to the complex matrix. JFETacLoad stamps 22 admittance terms with both real and imaginary parts: real conductances gdpr/gspr and state0 gm/gds/ggs/ggd (jfetacld.c:36-42,47-68), AND the omega-scaled junction-charge susceptances xgs = state0[JFETqgs]*CKTomega and xgd = state0[JFETqgd]*CKTomega stamped into the +1 (imaginary) cells (jfetacld.c:41,43,49,52,54,57,59,63,66). None of these are produced by digiTS. The sibling ported devices DO implement this: bjt.ts, mosfet.ts, vdmos.ts all have stampAc methods (njfet.ts absent). The jfet planning review (spec/v41-port/planning/jfet-review.md:60-62) FROZE the scope as 'DC operating point, transient, AND AC small-signal' and asserted (line 92) 'AC is implemented' for jfetacld.c; that assertion is FALSE for JFET (bad baseline / Tier-2 trap): no AC small-signal path exists in njfet.ts. The handles already exist in setup() (_hGG,_hGDP,_hGSP,_hDPG,_hDPDP,_hDPSP,_hSPG,_hSPDP,_hSPSP,_hDDP,_hDPD,_hDD,_hSSP,_hSPS,_hSS). After porting, re-verify h001 Tier 2 against jfetacld.c.
